title: Statisticians
description: Develop or apply mathematical or statistical theory and methods to collect, organize, interpret, and summarize numerical data to provide usable information. May specialize in fields such as biostatistics, agricultural statistics, business statistics, or economic statistics. Includes mathematical and survey statisticians.

tasks.text: Analyze and interpret statistical data to identify significant differences in relationships among sources of information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Evaluate the statistical methods and procedures used to obtain data to ensure validity, applicability, efficiency, and accuracy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Report results of statistical analyses, including information in the form of graphs, charts, and tables.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine whether statistical methods are appropriate, based on user needs or research questions of interest.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare data for processing by organizing information, checking for inaccuracies, and adjusting and weighting the raw data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and test experimental designs, sampling techniques, and analytical methods.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify relationships and trends in data, as well as any factors that could affect the results of research.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Present statistical and nonstatistical results, using charts, bullets, and graphs, in meetings or conferences to audiences such as clients, peers, and students.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design research projects that apply valid scientific techniques, and use information obtained from baselines or historical data to structure uncompromised and efficient analyses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Adapt statistical methods to solve specific problems in many fields, such as economics, biology, and engineering.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate sources of information to determine any limitations, in terms of reliability or usability.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Process large amounts of data for statistical modeling and graphic analysis, using computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop software applications or programming for statistical modeling and graphic analysis.
tasks.rationale: Software coding, programming, or application development task highly susceptible to automated generation.

tasks.text: Report results of statistical analyses in peer-reviewed papers and technical manuals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Plan data collection methods for specific projects, and determine the types and sizes of sample groups to be used.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply sampling techniques, or use complete enumeration bases to determine and define groups to be surveyed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine theories, such as those of probability and inference, to discover mathematical bases for new or improved methods of obtaining and evaluating numerical data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise and provide instructions for workers collecting and tabulating data.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and structure data warehouses for storing data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
